html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 冥通 - May refer to a mystical or spiritual connection; rendered as 'Dark Connection' or 'Mystical Link,' depending on context.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南朝梁 - Southern Dynasty of Liang, a period in Chinese history (502–557 CE).
  • 陶弘景 - Tao Hongjing, a prominent Daoist scholar and alchemist during the Southern Dynasty of Liang.
  • The term '冥通' could relate to Daoist or Buddhist concepts of the afterlife or the supernatural.

Proper Nouns

  • 冥通記 - Title of the text, rendered as 'Records of the Dark Connection' or 'Records of the Mystical Link.'
  • 南朝梁 - Rendered as 'Southern Dynasty of Liang' in English.
  • 陶弘景 - Rendered as 'Tao Hongjing' in English.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夏至 - Significant seasonal event in the Chinese calendar, marking the longest day of the year.
  • 曰未中 - Literally 'said not yet midday,' indicating it was still early in the day.
  • 少许在所住户南床眠 - Colloquial phrase meaning 'a little while later, I was lying on the bed in the south of my house.'
  • 始觉 - Means 'just beginning to feel awake' or 'starting to become aware.'
  • 仍令善生下帘 - Imperative phrase meaning 'still ordered Sheng to lower the curtain.'
  • 又眠未熟 - Means 'again, not yet fully asleep.'
  • 忽见一人 - Means 'suddenly saw a person.'
  • 长可七尺 - Measure of height, approximately 1.7 meters.
  • 面小，口鼻猛 - Describes a small face with prominent mouth and nose.
  • 眉多，少有须 - Means 'with many eyebrows and a few mustaches.'
  • 青白色 - Describes a color, likely a pale or bluish-white complexion.
  • 年可四十许 - Means 'around forty years old.'
  • 著朱衣赤帻 - Describes the person wearing red clothes and a red headdress.
  • 上戴蝉垂 - Means 'wearing a cicada pendant.'
  • 缨极长 - Describes a very long tassel.
  • 紫革带广七寸许 - Means 'a purple leather belt about seven inches wide.'
  • 带鞶囊 - Means 'with a pouch attached to the belt.'
  • 鞶囊作龙头 - Describes the pouch shaped like a dragon's head.
  • 足著两头舄 - Means 'wearing two-toed shoes.'
  • 舄紫色 - Describes the shoes as purple.
  • 行时有声，索索然 - Means 'made a sound when walking, like rustling.'
  • 从者十二人 - Means 'twelve attendants.'
  • 二人提裙 - Means 'two people holding up the skirt.'
  • 作两髻髻 - Describes the hairstyle, likely two braids.
  • 如永嘉老姥髻 - Compares the hairstyle to that of an elderly woman from Yongjia.
  • 紫衫，青裤 - Describes the clothing as purple shirts and blue pants.
  • 履缚裤极缓 - Means 'the shoes were tied loosely.'
  • 三人著紫裤，褶平巾帻 - Describes three people wearing purple pants and a flat cap.
  • 手各执简，简上有字，不可识 - Means 'each holding a tablet with writing that cannot be recognized.'
  • 又七人，并白布裤褶 - Means 'seven more people, all wearing white cloth pants and jackets.'
  • 自履褶悉有所执 - Means 'each holding something.'
  • 一人挟坐席 - Means 'one person carrying a sitting mat.'
  • 一人把如意五色毛扇 - Means 'one person holding a five-colored feather fan.'
  • 一人把大卷书 - Means 'one person holding a large scroll.'
  • 一人持纸笔大砚 - Means 'one person holding paper, pen, and a large inkstone.'
  • 砚黑色，笔犹如世上笔 - Describes the inkstone as black and the pen as similar to a common pen.
  • 一人捉伞 - Means 'one person holding an umbrella.'
  • 伞状如毛羽，又似彩帛，斑驳可爱 - Describes the umbrella as resembling feathers and colorful silk, with a speckled and attractive appearance.
  • 伞形圆深，柄黑色，极长 - Means 'the umbrella is round and deep, with a black handle that is very long.'
  • 入屋后，倚檐前 - Means 'after entering the house, leaning against the eaves.'
  • 其二人并持囊，囊大如小柱，似有文书 - Means 'two others holding bags, which are as large as small pillars and seem to contain documents.'
  • 挟席人舒置书床上 - Means 'the person carrying the mat spread it out on the bookshelf.'
  • 席白色，有光明 - Describes the mat as white and luminous.
  • 草缕如■〈艹邪〉子 - Describes the mat as having threads like grass.
  • 但伞缕尤大耳 - Means 'but the threads of the umbrella are particularly large.'
  • 侍者六人入户，并倚子平床前 - Means 'six attendants entered the house and leaned against the bed.'
  • 此人始人户，便皵面去，居太近，后仍就座，以臂隐书案 - Means 'this person, upon entering, wrinkled his face and moved closer, then sat down and hid his arm behind the desk.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 夏至 - A significant seasonal event in the Chinese calendar, marking the longest day of the year.
  • 永嘉老姥 - Refers to an elderly woman from Yongjia, a place name that may indicate a specific cultural or regional context.
  • 鞶囊 - A type of pouch or bag, often used in ancient Chinese attire.
  • 褶平巾帻 - A type of headgear, typically a flat cap.
  • 简 - Ancient tablets used for writing, often made of bamboo or wood.
  • 如意五色毛扇 - A decorative fan with five colors and feathers, often used in ceremonial or ritual contexts.
  • 大卷书 - A large scroll, often used for important documents or texts.
  • 纸笔大砚 - Common writing implements, including paper, pen, and inkstone.
  • 伞 - An umbrella, often used for protection from the sun or rain.
  • 草缕如■〈艹邪〉子 - A description of a mat made of grass threads, possibly a traditional or symbolic object.

Proper Nouns

  • 善生 - A proper name, likely referring to a specific person.
  • 永嘉 - A place name, possibly indicating a specific region or cultural context.
  • 鞶囊 - A type of pouch or bag, often used in ancient Chinese attire.
  • 褶平巾帻 - A type of headgear, typically a flat cap.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 于时笔及约尺，悉在案上 - Straightforward description; term '约尺' is unclear and may be a rare or local term.
  • 便自捉内格中 - '内格中' is ambiguous; could refer to a compartment or a frame.
  • 移格置北头 - Phrase is clear in structure but may refer to a specific room or setting requiring contextual clarification.
  • 那不将几来？ - '几' is polysemous and requires careful contextual interpretation; can mean 'table' or 'bench'.
  • 嘉卿无愆，故来相造 - Standard classical expression of commendation and visitation.
  • 依常朝拜中食耳 - '朝拜' could be religious, but in daily routine context may refer to ritual or practice.
  • 未晓斋法 - Refers to a specific religious or ascetic practice, possibly Buddhist or Daoist fasting.
  • 中食亦足，但夏月眠不益人，莫恒贪眠 - Straightforward advisory statement; tone is didactic.
  • 体羸，有小事竟觉倦，倦如欲眠，不能自禁 - Clear expression of physical weakness and fatigue.
  • 小小消息无苦 - Colloquial expression of reassurance.
  • 赤豆在庭中成走来 - Phrase is problematic; may be a misreading or poetic expression. '赤豆' is clear as 'red beans,' but the rest is ambiguous.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 嘉卿无愆，故来相造 - Standard form of classical Chinese expression used in official or literary contexts to commend someone and justify a visit.
  • 斋法 - May refer to a Buddhist or Daoist fasting ritual; common ascetic practice during Southern and Northern Dynasties.
  • 赤豆 - Common food item in ancient China; may be symbolic in literature or poetry.

Proper Nouns

  • 子良 - Name, possibly referring to a historical figure; transliterated as 'Zi Liang' or 'Ziliang'.
  • 山府丞 - Title: 'Deputy of the Mountain Office' or 'Assistant to the Mountain Governor'; non-standard term.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 勿令裸身 - Command to keep the child clothed.
  • 得值正法 - Encountered the Dharma, a concept in Buddhism referring to the teachings of the Buddha.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钱塘 - Refers to Qiantang, a city in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 永嘉 - Refers to Yongjia, another city in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 陶先生 - Likely refers to a respected scholar or official named Tao.
  • 王家 - Refers to the royal family, indicating a high social status.
  • 正法 - Refers to the Dharma, a concept in Buddhism referring to the teachings of the Buddha.

Proper Nouns

  • 郎善 - A proper name.
  • 子良 - A proper name.
  • 陶先生 - A title and name.
  • 王家 - A title referring to the royal family.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夫生为人，实依依于世上 - Formal opening phrase meaning 'As for living as a human, one is indeed attached to the world.'
  • 死为神，则恋恋于幽冥 - Means 'After death, one becomes a spirit and lingers in the underworld.'
  • 实而论之，幽冥为胜 - Concessive statement meaning 'Speaking frankly, the underworld is superior.'
  • 府中缺一任，欲以卿补之 - Means 'There is a vacancy in the office, and I wish to appoint you to fill it.'
  • 事目将定，莫复多言 - Means 'The matter is nearly decided, so do not speak further.'
  • 来年十月当相召，可逆管办具 - Means 'In October next year, I will summon you, so prepare accordingly.'
  • 若不从此命者，则三官符至 - Means 'If you do not obey this command, the three officials' edict will come.'
  • 可不慎之 - Means 'You must be cautious about it.'
  • 予良便有惧色 - Means 'I immediately showed a look of fear.'
  • 卿趣欲住世种罪 - Means 'You seem to want to remain in the world and commit sins.'
  • 何为得补吾洞中之职 - Means 'Why should you be appointed to a position in my cave?'
  • 自计天下，无胜此处 - Means 'Considering the world, there is no place better than this.'
  • 唯仰由耳 - Means 'I will follow your instructions.'
  • 卿自幼至今，不无小愆 - Means 'Since childhood, you have not been without minor faults.'
  • 可自思悔谢 - Means 'You should reflect and repent.'
  • 若不尔者，亦为身累 - Means 'If you do not, it will also burden you.'
  • 凡修道者，皆不裸身露髻 - Means 'All who practice the Way do not expose their bodies or hair.'
  • 枉滥无辜，起止饮食，悉应依科 - Means 'They do not wrong the innocent and follow the rules in all actions and meals.'
  • 卿复相告，言穷于此 - Means 'I have told you all I can.'
  • 今还所任，方事犹疑，冀非远耳 - Means 'Now that you have taken on the position, you still have doubts, but it is not far away.'
  • 卿勖吾言，勿示世中 - Means 'Take my words to heart and do not reveal them to the world.'
  • 悠悠之人，山中同气，知之无嫌 - Means 'Those who are indifferent, those in the mountains who share the same breath, will not be offended if they know.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 幽冥 - Refers to the underworld or the afterlife.
  • 三官 - Refers to the Three Officials, deities in Taoist cosmology.
  • 洞中之职 - Refers to a position in a cave, likely a metaphor for a spiritual or hidden realm.
  • 天真 - Refers to the true nature or essence, often used in Taoist contexts.
  • 圣府 - Refers to the sacred court or a place of spiritual authority.
  • 修道者 - Refers to those who practice the Way, often in a Taoist or spiritual context.
  • 裸身露髻 - Refers to exposing one's body or hair, which is discouraged in spiritual practice.
  • 枉滥无辜 - Refers to wronging the innocent, a moral principle in Chinese philosophy.

Proper Nouns

  • 三官 - The Three Officials, deities in Taoist cosmology.
  • 洞中之职 - A position in a cave, likely a metaphor for a spiritual or hidden realm.
  • 天真 - The true nature or essence, often used in Taoist contexts.
  • 圣府 - The sacred court or a place of spiritual authority.
  • 修道者 - Those who practice the Way, often in a Taoist or spiritual context.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 便下席，未出户 - Literally 'descended from the mat, not yet out the door,' describing a quick movement or action.
  • 勿令小儿辈逼坛靖 - Do not let the young ones approach the altar of tranquility.
  • 不可即除 - It cannot be removed immediately.
  • 腹中亦有卒可差 - There is also a sudden improvement in the abdomen.
  • 令春等去，便下阶而灭 - After Lingchun and others left, he descended the steps and disappeared.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 坛靖 - Likely refers to a sacred or ritual space, possibly related to religious or spiritual practices.
  • 真经 - Refers to genuine or authentic scriptures, possibly religious texts.
  • 茅君 - Likely refers to a master or leader, possibly in a religious or spiritual context.
  • 中山 - Refers to a place, possibly a region or city.
  • 范帅 - Likely refers to a commander or leader, possibly in a military context.

Proper Nouns

  • 令春 - A specific name, likely referring to an individual.
  • 刘白 - A specific name, likely referring to an individual.
  • 卿姨 - A specific person, likely referring to a female relative.
  • 子良 - A specific person, likely referring to an individual.
